channel.title: KiddieDaily — Science
channel.description: Kid-friendly science and nature news, bias-rated.

item.title: Have scientists really made a living cell from scratch? Not quite
item.description: A prototype cell partly capable of replicating itself has been created using 36 existing bacterial genes, but it's not really a living organism – yet

item.title: The 5 Types of Aliens People Most Frequently Say They See
item.description: From the Greys to the Nordics, these aliens appear frequently across abduction stories and science fiction tales.

item.title: What’s Up: July 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA
item.description: A predawn Moon-and-planets meetup, a returning comet, a great chance to see the Milky Way, and Saturn’s rings at a new angle. Skywatching Highlights Transcript An early morning hangout with the Moon a

item.title: New Indonesia roadmap aims to protect Indigenous knowledge for biodiversity
item.description: JAKARTA — The Indonesian government is developing a roadmap to protect local wisdom in biodiversity conservation, a move aimed at strengthening the recognition and protection of Indigenous peoples and

item.title: Colorful songbirds face higher risk of extinction
item.description: While prized as pets in some places, the pet trade is not entirely to blame. The post Colorful songbirds face higher risk of extinction appeared first on Popular Science .

item.title: How much will Americans spend on Fourth of July food this year?
item.description: Ten of the 12 Fourth of July staples tracked by the American Farm Bureau Federation cost more than they did a year ago.

item.title: Boy dies from rabies after waking up to bat on his face
item.description: According to a journal report, the unnamed boy died several weeks after the bat landed on his face while on vacation in Ontario.

item.title: Mexico: The 2026 World Cup's best host?
item.description: Mexico has fully embraced its role as a World Cup host. Many visitors rave about the atmosphere and wish there were more knockout-stage matches in Mexico as opposed to the United States.

item.title: Good Morning, Earth!
item.description: NASA astronaut Chris Williams took this photo of an orbital sunrise from the International Space Station on June 26, 2026. In 24 hours, the space station makes 16 orbits of Earth, traveling through 16

item.title: NASA’s Webb Reveals Stars Sparking to Life in Cosmic Celebration
item.description: NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope has captured the infrared light of numerous features that previously were impossible to see beyond the thick dust of the FS Tau star system. In addition to myriad bac

item.title: The natural history of every U.S. state is on display at a new D.C. exhibit
item.description: The Smithsonian Natural History Museum’s latest exhibit, “From These Lands,” connects visitors with America’s natural history.

item.title: The animal behind most aggressive wildlife encounters may surprise you
item.description: Analysis of 3,000 incidents in Canada reveals which animal–human activity combos are especially risky. Of note: Elk and campgrounds are a bad mix.

item.title: Complex carbon in Mars rocks: Another clue to past life?
item.description: Scientists have found complex carbon in Mars rocks that the Perseverance rover has studied. They're the same rocks found in 2024 to contain hints of life. The post Complex carbon in Mars rocks: Anothe

item.title: Exquisite Albireo, a beloved and colorful double star
item.description: Albireo, in the constellation Cygnus, is a favorite for stargazers. Through a small telescope, it appears as a beautiful gold star with a dimmer blue companion. The post Exquisite Albireo, a beloved a

item.title: James Webb telescope may have discovered a mysterious, never-before-seen substance on Pluto and Titan
item.description: A new study has identified a very specific wavelength of light missing from both Pluto and Saturn's largest moon, Titan. The surprising signal suggests that these worlds harbor an unknown molecule tha

item.title: Ancient ring discovered underground in Scotland could be a Stonehenge-like monument
item.description: A hidden ring of stones or timbers detected beneath peat at Machrie Moor could represent a previously unknown Neolithic or Bronze Age monument.

item.title: Don't whitewash American history on the Fourth of July
item.description: Frederick Douglass's 1852 Fourth of July speech highlighted the hypocrisy of celebrating independence while slavery still existed, and today Black Americans still face economic and social discriminati

item.title: Northern lights could dazzle to start July 4 weekend
item.description: Sure, you may be hoping to see fireworks or a drone show over the July 4 weekend, but nature may be gearing up to give you its own colorful nighttime display.

item.title: Earth Might Be Home to 20 Million Insect Species—More Than Three Times as Many as Previously Thought, a Study Suggests
item.description: Recent estimates have come to the consensus that our planet hosts roughly six million insect species. New research reveals that they may have drastically underestimated their counts

item.title: Man converts canoe into a sailboat, takes it for a spin
item.description: No drilling required. The post Man converts canoe into a sailboat, takes it for a spin appeared first on Popular Science .
